hypotheses.summary: Dual-antenna moving-base RTK never produces a carrier-phase solution because the rover receiver never ingests the moving-base's observation stream (UBX-RXM-RAWX/SFRBX or RTCM3 4072.0/4072.1 + 1077/1087/1097/1127); the rover is effectively in a permanent wait-for-base-observations state.

evidence.snippet: carr_soln=none, rel_pos_valid=0, rel_pos_heading_valid=0 on first sample and every one of 18133 samples over 3626.8 s
flags=223 (diffSoln=1) in 16637/16639 samples -- MB itself receives NTRIP corrections cleanly, so the failure is downstream of MB
flags=221 (diffSoln=0) in 15418/18133 samples -- rover only opportunistically picks up RTCM (likely leaked from NTRIP) and never from the MB link
ublox_rover TMODE3 Not configured; ublox_moving_base TMODE3 Not configured -- receiver role/port configuration did not stick
h_acc stays in 469-1295 mm band (DGPS-float territory) for the whole run; never dips into the 10-30 mm band that RTK fixed would produce

hypotheses.patch_hint: Fix the MB->rover inter-receiver UART path: configure MB UART2 to output UBX-RXM-RAWX/SFRBX (or RTCM3 4072.0, 4072.1, 1077, 1087, 1097, 1127, 1230) at matching baud, configure rover UART2 as RTCM3 input, disable TMODE3 on both, and verify TX/RX wiring is not swapped.

hypotheses.summary: Drive-by-wire is never engaged during the session, so the operator's premise of 'behavior degradation' is not supported: there is no autonomous control loop whose behavior could have degraded.

evidence.snippet: brake.enabled has exactly one state transition in the log (at t=0.054 s to value 0) and remains 0 for all 181k+ samples
throttle.enabled likewise stays at 0 for all 362k+ samples
brake.enabled=0 during the alleged tunnel-entry window -- the vehicle was under manual control when GPS degraded
ekf_se_map 'No events recorded' at t=0.40 s -- the map-frame pose fuser never ran, so no autonomy module had a localized pose to act on

hypotheses.patch_hint: Either the operator narrative is mislabeled (this was a manual recon drive, not an autonomous run) or the DBW-enable handshake failed at session start; add a pre-flight check that refuses to record a 'run' bag unless brake.enabled and throttle.enabled are observed to go 1 before motion begins.

hypotheses.summary: Operator hypothesis REFUTED: the Sanfer tunnel entry did cause mild GNSS degradation (num_sv 29->16, h_acc 645 mm -> 1294 mm between t=2621.4 s and t=2681.2 s) but it did NOT cause an RTK/heading failure and could not have caused any behavior change because DBW was never engaged; the dominant localization failure (RTK heading = none) is session-wide and starts 43 minutes before the tunnel.

evidence.snippet: num_sv=19, h_acc=799 mm at tunnel onset (mild, still 3D fix)
worst in-tunnel sample: num_sv=16, h_acc=1294 mm, fix_type=3 -- no fix-loss
carr_soln=none ALREADY at t=0.24 s (43 min pre-tunnel) -- tunnel cannot be the cause of a pre-existing condition
brake.enabled=0 throughout the tunnel window -- no autonomous behavior was active to degrade

hypotheses.patch_hint: Re-interview operator: the 'tunnel anomaly' perception is likely an after-the-fact attribution. The real issue is the session-wide RTK configuration fault; fixing that will also make future tunnel traversals observable (you will see carr_soln go fixed -> float -> none -> float -> fixed around the tunnel instead of a flat 'none').

hypotheses.summary: Independent secondary defect: the Dataspeed DBW NMEA GPS parser emits /vehicle/gps/fix with latitude frozen at ~-3e-7 deg for all 3591 messages while longitude is correct (~-58.53). Latitude field is silently zeroed, likely a sign/hemisphere or DDMM.MMMM decode bug with no validity guard.

evidence.snippet: lat range over all 3591 msgs: [-3e-07, -3e-07]; lon range: [-58.571, -58.507]; status=0 always
last sample: lat=-3e-07, lon=-58.530592 -- same frozen-lat pattern at end of run

hypotheses.patch_hint: In the NMEA $GPGGA lat parsing, reject/NaN the field if it does not pass range + hemisphere-character validation; do not publish NavSatFix with a silently-zeroed latitude.

hypotheses.summary: Two NTRIP correction starvation events (20 s around t=1664 s, 4+ s around t=3347 s) briefly reduce MB correction quality, but are not the root cause: they postdate the RTK failure and MB still reports diffSoln=1 through/around them.

evidence.snippet: /ntrip_client: Unable to send NMEA sentence to server / Exception: timed out
NMEA sentence failed to send to server 5 times, restarting; RTCM requested before client was connected, returning empty list
/ntrip_client level=8: RTCM data not received for 4 seconds, reconnecting

hypotheses.patch_hint: Lower the NTRIP reconnect threshold from 5 failed NMEA sends to 2 and enable exponential backoff capped at 2 s; suppress repetitive checksum-mismatch logs.

payload.patch_proposal:
--- a/ublox_gps/config/moving_base.yaml
+++ b/ublox_gps/config/moving_base.yaml
@@
-rate: 8                  # Hz, drove 'Frequency too high' diag at 0.52 s then 'too low' at 195.39 s
-tmode3: 1                # survey-in -- wrong for a MOVING base, flagged by diagnostics
+rate: 5                  # Hz, matches u-blox driver watchdog band for the fix topic
+tmode3: 0                # DISABLED -- moving base must not be in survey-in / fixed mode
 
 uart2:
-  baudrate: 115200
-  in_protocol:  ubx
-  out_protocol: ubx        # <-- MB was NOT emitting RAWX/SFRBX to the rover
+  baudrate: 460800
+  in_protocol:  none
+  out_protocol: ubx+rtcm3
+  # Enable the messages the rover needs to form a carrier-phase baseline:
+  out_msgs:
+    - {id: UBX-RXM-RAWX,  rate: 1}
+    - {id: UBX-RXM-SFRBX, rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-4072.0,  rate: 1}   # u-blox moving-base reference station
+    - {id: RTCM3-4072.1,  rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-1077,    rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-1087,    rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-1097,    rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-1127,    rate: 1}
+    - {id: RTCM3-1230,    rate: 10}  # GLONASS code-phase biases, every 10 s

--- a/ublox_gps/config/rover.yaml
+++ b/ublox_gps/config/rover.yaml
@@
-tmode3: 1
-dgnss_mode: 2            # RTK_FLOAT only
+tmode3: 0
+dgnss_mode: 3            # RTK_FIXED -- attempt ambiguity resolution
 
 uart2:
-  baudrate: 115200
-  in_protocol:  ubx
+  baudrate: 460800        # MUST match moving_base.uart2.baudrate
+  in_protocol:  ubx+rtcm3 # accept MB observations (UBX-RXM-*) and/or RTCM3 4072/107x
   out_protocol: none
 
 publish:
   nav:
     relposned: true
     heading:   true
-    pvt:       true
+    pvt:       true   # keep, but the heading topic is what /ekf_se_map should fuse

--- a/ntrip_client/params.yaml
+++ b/ntrip_client/params.yaml
@@
-nmea_max_send_failures: 5
-reconnect_backoff_s:    20
+nmea_max_send_failures: 2
+reconnect_backoff_s:    2
+reconnect_backoff_cap_s: 8
+rtcm_crc24q_validate:   true
+drop_bad_packets_silently: true   # ~100 checksum-mismatch lines spammed rosout this run

--- a/launch/localization.launch.xml
+++ b/launch/localization.launch.xml
@@
+  <!-- Preflight: refuse to start if MB<->rover link is not up after 10 s -->
+  <node pkg="gnss_preflight" exec="mb_rover_link_check" name="mb_rover_link_check">
+    <param name="timeout_s"             value="10.0"/>
+    <param name="require_carr_soln_min" value="float"/>   <!-- at least float within 10 s of clear sky -->
+    <param name="fail_fast"             value="true"/>
+  </node>

--- a/robot_localization/ekf_se_map.yaml
+++ b/robot_localization/ekf_se_map.yaml
@@
-# imu0/odom0/pose0 wiring assumed ublox_rover/fix + /imu/data
-# (diagnostic at t=0.40 s reports 'No events recorded' for the entire run)
+# Fix: subscribe explicitly to the dual-antenna heading topic so the EKF
+# does not silently wait for an input that never arrives.
+pose1: /ublox_rover/navheading
+pose1_config: [false, false, false,  false, false, true,   false, false, false,  false, false, false,  false, false, false]
+pose1_differential: false
+pose1_relative:    false
+pose1_queue_size:  5

--- a/dbw_mkz/src/nmea_gps_parser.cpp
+++ b/dbw_mkz/src/nmea_gps_parser.cpp
@@
-  msg.latitude = parse_ddmm(fields[2]);         // hemisphere char ignored -> silently 0 on malformed input
-  // no validity guard
+  double lat;
+  if (!try_parse_ddmm(fields[2], fields[3], &lat) || std::abs(lat) < 1e-3) {
+    msg.latitude  = std::numeric_limits<double>::quiet_NaN();
+    msg.status.status = sensor_msgs::msg::NavSatStatus::STATUS_NO_FIX;
+    return;
+  }
+  msg.latitude = lat;
